heading: Property exempt from levy

State Fish and Wildlife Permits, , , provided that:Pub. L. 105–206, title III, § 3445(c)112 Stat. 763July 22, 1998“(1).—In generalWith respect to permits issued by a State and required under State law for the harvest of fish or wildlife in the trade or business of an individual taxpayer, the term ‘other assets’ as used in section 6334(e)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall include future income which may be derived by such taxpayer from the commercial sale of fish or wildlife under such permit.“(2).—ConstructionParagraph (1) shall not be construed to invalidate or in any way prejudice any assertion that the privilege embodied in permits described in paragraph (1) is not property or a right to property under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.”

L. 105–206, title III, § 3445(c)112 Stat. 763July 22, 1998“(1).—In generalWith respect to permits issued by a State and required under State law for the harvest of fish or wildlife in the trade or business of an individual taxpayer, the term ‘other assets’ as used in section 6334(e)(2) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall include future income which may be derived by such taxpayer from the commercial sale of fish or wildlife under such permit.“(2).—ConstructionParagraph (1) shall not be construed to invalidate or in any way prejudice any assertion that the privilege embodied in permits described in paragraph (1) is not property or a right to property under the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.”

children.heading: Wearing apparel and school books
children.label: 1 Wearing apparel and school books
children.text: Such items of wearing apparel and such school books as are necessary for the taxpayer or for members of his family;

children.heading: Fuel, provisions, furniture, and personal effects
children.label: 2 Fuel, provisions, furniture, and personal effects
children.text: So much of the fuel, provisions, furniture, and personal effects in the taxpayer’s household, and of the arms for personal use, livestock, and poultry of the taxpayer, as does not exceed $6,250 in value;

children.heading: Books and tools of a trade, business, or profession
children.label: 3 Books and tools of a trade, business, or profession
children.text: So many of the books and tools necessary for the trade, business, or profession of the taxpayer as do not exceed in the aggregate $3,125 in value.

children.heading: Unemployment benefits
children.label: 4 Unemployment benefits
children.text: Any amount payable to an individual with respect to his unemployment (including any portion thereof payable with respect to dependents) under an unemployment compensation law of the United States, of any State, or of the District of Columbia or of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Undelivered mail
children.label: 5 Undelivered mail
children.text: Mail, addressed to any person, which has not been delivered to the addressee.

children.heading: Certain annuity and pension payments
children.label: 6 Certain annuity and pension payments
children.text: Annuity or pension payments under the Railroad Retirement Act, benefits under the Railroad Unemployment Insurance Act, special pension payments received by a person whose name has been entered on the Army, Navy, Air Force, and Coast Guard Medal of Honor roll (), and annuities based on retired or retainer pay under chapter 73 of title 10 of the United States Code.38 U.S.C. 1562

children.heading: Workmen’s compensation
children.label: 7 Workmen’s compensation
children.text: Any amount payable to an individual as workmen’s compensation (including any portion thereof payable with respect to dependents) under a workmen’s compensation law of the United States, any State, the District of Columbia, or the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Judgments for support of minor children
children.label: 8 Judgments for support of minor children
children.text: If the taxpayer is required by judgment of a court of competent jurisdiction, entered prior to the date of levy, to contribute to the support of his minor children, so much of his salary, wages, or other income as is necessary to comply with such judgment.

children.heading: Minimum exemption for wages, salary, and other income
children.label: 9 Minimum exemption for wages, salary, and other income
children.text: Any amount payable to or received by an individual as wages or salary for personal services, or as income derived from other sources, during any period, to the extent that the total of such amounts payable to or received by him during such period does not exceed the applicable exempt amount determined under subsection (d).

children.label: A
children.text: subchapter II, III, IV, V, or VI of chapter 11 of such title 38, or

children.label: B
children.text: chapter 13, 21, 23, 31, 32, 34, 35, 37, or 39 of such title 38.

children.label: A
children.text: title IV or title XVI (relating to supplemental security income for the aged, blind, and disabled) of the Social Security Act, or

children.label: B
children.text: State or local government public assistance or public welfare programs for which eligibility is determined by a needs or income test.

children.heading: Assistance under Job Training Partnership Act
children.label: 12 Assistance under Job Training Partnership Act
children.text: Any amount payable to a participant under the Job Training Partnership Act ( et seq.) from funds appropriated pursuant to such Act.29 U.S.C. 1501

children.heading: Residences exempt in small deficiency cases and principal residences and certain business assets exempt in absence of certain approval or jeopardy
children.label: 13 Residences exempt in small deficiency cases and principal residences and certain business assets exempt in absence of certain approval or jeopardy

children.heading: Residences in small deficiency cases
children.label: A Residences in small deficiency cases
i
children.text: any real property used as a residence by the taxpayer; or

children.label: ii
children.text: any real property of the taxpayer (other than real property which is rented) used by any other individual as a residence.

children.heading: Principal residences and certain business assets
children.label: B Principal residences and certain business assets
i
children.text: the principal residence of the taxpayer (within the meaning of section 121); and

children.label: ii
children.text: tangible personal property or real property (other than real property which is rented) used in the trade or business of an individual taxpayer.

children.heading: Appraisal
children.label: b Appraisal
children.text: The officer seizing property of the type described in subsection (a) shall appraise and set aside to the owner the amount of such property declared to be exempt. If the taxpayer objects at the time of the seizure to the valuation fixed by the officer making the seizure, the Secretary shall summon three disinterested individuals who shall make the valuation.

children.heading: No other property exempt
children.label: c No other property exempt
children.text: Notwithstanding any other law of the United States (including section 207 of the Social Security Act), no property or rights to property shall be exempt from levy other than the property specifically made exempt by subsection (a).

children.heading: Exempt amount of wages, salary, or other income
children.label: d Exempt amount of wages, salary, or other income

children.heading: Individuals on weekly basis
children.label: 1 Individuals on weekly basis
children.text: In the case of an individual who is paid or receives all of his wages, salary, and other income on a weekly basis, the amount of the wages, salary, and other income payable to or received by him during any week which is exempt from levy under subsection (a)(9) shall be the exempt amount.

children.label: i
children.text: the standard deduction, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the aggregate amount of the deductions for personal exemptions allowed the taxpayer under section 151 in the taxable year in which such levy occurs, divided by

children.heading: Individuals on basis other than weekly
children.label: 3 Individuals on basis other than weekly
children.text: In the case of any individual not described in paragraph (1), the amount of the wages, salary, and other income payable to or received by him during any applicable pay period or other fiscal period (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary) which is exempt from levy under subsection (a)(9) shall be an amount (determined under such regulations) which as nearly as possible will result in the same total exemption from levy for such individual over a period of time as he would have under paragraph (1) if (during such period of time) he were paid or received such wages, salary, and other income on a regular weekly basis.

children.heading: Years when personal exemption amount is zero
children.label: 4 Years when personal exemption amount is zero
i
children.text: the sum of the amount determined under subparagraph (B) and the standard deduction, divided by

children.heading: Amount determined
children.label: B Amount determined
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the amount determined under this subparagraph is $4,150 multiplied by the number of the taxpayer’s dependents for the taxable year in which the levy occurs.

children.label: i
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “2017” for “2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Verified statement
children.label: D Verified statement
children.text: Unless the taxpayer submits to the Secretary a written and properly verified statement specifying the facts necessary to determine the proper amount under subparagraph (A), subparagraph (A) shall be applied as if the taxpayer were a married individual filing a separate return with no dependents.

children.heading: Levy allowed on principal residences and certain business assets in certain circumstances
children.label: e Levy allowed on principal residences and certain business assets in certain circumstances

children.heading: Approval required
children.label: A Approval required
children.text: A principal residence shall not be exempt from levy if a judge or magistrate of a district court of the United States approves (in writing) the levy of such residence.

children.heading: Jurisdiction
children.label: B Jurisdiction
children.text: The district courts of the United States shall have exclusive jurisdiction to approve a levy under subparagraph (A).

children.label: A
children.text: a district director or assistant district director of the Internal Revenue Service personally approves (in writing) the levy of such property; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary finds that the collection of tax is in jeopardy.

children.heading: Levy allowed on certain specified payments
children.label: f Levy allowed on certain specified payments
children.text: Any payment described in subparagraph (B) or (C) of section 6331(h)(2) shall not be exempt from levy if the Secretary approves the levy thereon under section 6331(h).

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for such calendar year, by substituting “calendar year 1998” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Rounding
children.label: 2 Rounding
children.text: If any dollar amount after being increased under paragraph (1) is not a multiple of $10, such dollar amount shall be rounded to the nearest multiple of $10.
